tools.when_to_use: Use for almost all steps where the user needs to find or interact with a specific object. This is the default tool.
tools.when_not_to_use: Do not use when the user needs to wait, move in a direction, or perform a precise hand gesture.

settings.name: placement_rule
settings.description: STRICT RULE: If the object is viewed entirely from the front (like a monitor, button, microwave face, cabinet door, or wall-mounted item), you MUST use 'front'. Use 'up' ONLY for objects resting on tables/floors where the top surface is clearly visible.

tools.when_to_use: Use when an object is being relocated from one specific point to another specific destination.
tools.when_not_to_use: Do not use if the destination is unknown or off-screen.

settings.name: role
settings.description: 'source' is the object being moved. 'target' is where it goes.

settings.name: placement_rule
settings.description: Where to anchor the endpoints. Use up ONLY when the top of the object is VISIBLE and AVAILABLE for an overlay, otherwise use FRONT.

tools.when_to_use: Use when the user must cut, slice, dice, mince, or chop an item with a blade.
tools.when_not_to_use: Do not use for picking up, moving, or wiping objects.

settings.name: placement_rule
settings.description: Always placed floating just above the top face of the item.

tools.when_to_use: Use when the user must move, pull, push, slide, or rotate something in a specific direction. Example: 'slide the drawer outward', 'push the lever down'.
tools.when_not_to_use: Do not use to highlight a stationary object. Do not use for waiting steps.

settings.name: world_direction
settings.description: The direction the arrow points in world space, relative to the user's forward vector at capture time.

settings.name: placement_rule
settings.description: Which face of the detected object the arrow is anchored to before pointing outward.

tools.when_to_use: Use when the step requires physical hand interaction. Choose 'poke' to press buttons, tap, or flip switches. Choose 'grab' ONLY to physically pick up, carry, or take hold of an object. Choose 'clean' ONLY when friction is applied to a surface, such as wiping, scrubbing, dusting, or sweeping. Never use 'clean' for picking items up.Choose 'rotate' for turning knobs, twisting caps, or spinning dials.
tools.when_not_to_use: Do not use for simple navigation steps or directional movement with no hand action.

settings.name: gesture
settings.description: 'poke' — fingertip forward, aimed at the object. 'grab' — open palm forward, aimed at the object. 'clean' — palm facing the user.'rotate' — gripping and twisting motion.

settings.name: placement_rule
settings.description: STRICT RULES: If gesture is 'grab', this MUST be 'up'. If gesture is 'clean', this MUST be 'up'. If gesture is 'poke', this MUST be 'front'.If gesture is 'rotate', use 'front' for wall/panel knobs, or 'up' for jar caps.

tools.when_to_use: Use when the user must wait for a specific duration: cooking timers, drying times, charging waits, curing times.
tools.when_not_to_use: Do not use unless a specific duration is mentioned or strongly implied. Do not use for vague waits like 'let it cool' or 'wait until done'.

settings.name: duration_seconds
settings.description: Duration to count down in seconds. Convert minutes to seconds (e.g. 5 minutes = 300).

settings.name: placement_rule
settings.description: Where to place the timer relative to the detected object. Use 'floating' only if no object was detected for this step.

tools.when_to_use: Use for identification steps: 'this is the HDMI port', 'the red wire goes here'. Also use for query answers where you point to a specific object.
tools.when_not_to_use: Do not use when the user needs to perform an action — use indicator_arrow instead.

settings.name: text
settings.description: The short label to display. Maximum 40 characters.

settings.name: placement_rule
settings.description: Which face of the detected object the label pin appears on.
